name: The Sun
description: The Sun is the star at the center of our solar system.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are looking at the visible surface of the Sun. Darker areas may be sunspots, which are cooler magnetic regions.
whyItIsInteresting: The Sun is our nearest star and it changes from day to day, making it a great beginner target.

name: The Moon
description: The Moon is Earth’s closest neighbor in space and one of the best beginner photography targets.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are seeing sunlight reflecting off the Moon’s surface, including craters, plains, and rugged terrain.
whyItIsInteresting: The Moon changes appearance night by night, making it easy to revisit and photograph often.

name: Messier 3
shortDescription: A dense ball of ancient stars.
description: M3 is a globular cluster, a huge spherical group of old stars packed closely together.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are seeing many ancient stars held together by gravity in a tight, round cluster.
whyItIsInteresting: Globular clusters are some of the oldest objects in our galaxy, giving us a glimpse into the Milky Way’s early history.
captureNotes: Captured as a beginner deep-sky target using the Seestar S30.

name: The Beehive Cluster
description: The Beehive Cluster is a group of stars that formed together and still travel through space as a loose family.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are seeing a real group of stars that are physically related, not just stars that happen to line up from our view.
whyItIsInteresting: M44 is bright, easy to understand visually, and a great beginner example of an open star cluster.

name: The Whirlpool Galaxy
shortDescription: A spiral galaxy interacting with a smaller neighbor.
description: M51, the Whirlpool Galaxy, is a beautiful spiral galaxy with a smaller companion galaxy nearby.
whatYouAreSeeing: It's Interacting With Another Galaxy The small galaxy visible next to M51 is NGC 5195. These two galaxies are gravitationally interacting. NGC 5195 has likely passed through or near M51 several times over hundreds of millions of years. That interaction is believed to have helped create M51's spectacular spiral pattern. You're essentially photographing a cosmic traffic accident in slow motion. The interaction with NGC 5195 compresses gas clouds in M51's spiral arms. Those compressedclouds collapse and form new stars, creating bright blue star-forming regions throughout the galaxy. The blue patches you sometimes see in detailed photos are areas where young, hot stars are being born.
whyItIsInteresting: Like most large galaxies, M51 contains a supermassive black hole at its center. Astronomers estimate it has a mass of roughly a million times the mass of the Sun. he interaction with NGC 5195 compresses gas clouds in M51's spiral arms. Those compressed clouds collapse and form new stars, creating bright blue star-forming regions throughout the galaxy. The blue patches you sometimes see in detailed photos are areas where young, hot stars are being born.

name: Messier 65
shortDescription: A spiral galaxy in the Leo Triplet.
description: M65 is a spiral galaxy and part of a small group of galaxies known as the Leo Triplet.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are seeing a distant island of stars far beyond our Milky Way.
whyItIsInteresting: M65 helps show that galaxies come in groups and that the universe is filled with huge star systems beyond our own.

name: Bode’s Galaxy
description: M81, also called Bode’s Galaxy, is a large spiral galaxy and one of the brighter galaxies visible to amateur astronomers.
whyItIsInteresting: M81 is a great beginner galaxy because it is relatively bright and has a classic spiral galaxy shape.

name: The Owl Nebula
shortDescription: A planetary nebula with an owl-like appearance.
description: M97, the Owl Nebula, is a shell of gas released by a dying star.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are seeing glowing gas that was pushed out by an aging star near the end of its life.
whyItIsInteresting: Despite the name, a planetary nebula has nothing to do with planets. It shows one possible future stage in the life of a star like the Sun.

name: The Sombrero Galaxy
shortDescription: A galaxy with a bright center and dark dust lane.
description: M104, the Sombrero Galaxy, is famous for its bright central bulge and dark band of dust that gives it a hat-like shape.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are seeing a distant galaxy tilted in a way that makes its dust lane stand out clearly.
whyItIsInteresting: M104 is one of the most recognizable galaxies because of its unusual shape and strong contrast.

name: The Needle Galaxy
shortDescription: A thin edge-on spiral galaxy viewed from the side.
description: NGC 4565 is an edge-on spiral galaxy often called the Needle Galaxy.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are seeing a spiral galaxy from the side, which makes it appear as a long, thin line.
whyItIsInteresting: It helps show that spiral galaxies are flat disks, and their appearance depends on the angle we view them from.

name: Sirius
shortDescription: The brightest star in the night sky.
description: Sirius is the brightest star visible in Earth’s night sky.
whatYouAreSeeing: You are seeing a very bright nearby star, much closer than the galaxies and clusters in this gallery.
whyItIsInteresting: Sirius is a great reminder that not every space target is a galaxy or nebula. Some are individual stars that stand out because they are close and bright.
